_comment: Lair Actions and Regional Effects for SRD 5.1 monsters. Keyed by monster slug (matches 5e-database index). Adult and Ancient dragons of the same color share lair/regional data per SRD.

aboleth.lair_actions_intro: When fighting inside its lair, an aboleth can invoke the ambient magic to take lair actions. On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the aboleth takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the aboleth can't use the same effect two rounds in a row.

lair_actions.name: Phantasmal Force
lair_actions.desc: The aboleth casts phantasmal force (no components required) on any number of creatures it can see within 60 feet of it. While maintaining concentration on this effect, the aboleth can't take other lair actions. If a target succeeds on the saving throw or if the effect ends for it, the target is immune to the aboleth's phantasmal force lair action for the next 24 hours, although such a creature can choose to be affected.

lair_actions.name: Grasping Pool
lair_actions.desc: Pools of water within 90 feet of the aboleth surge outward in a grasping tide. Any creature on the ground within 20 feet of such a pool must succeed on a DC 14 Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 20 feet into the water and knocked prone. The aboleth can't use this lair action again until it has used a different one.

lair_actions.name: Slime Curse
lair_actions.desc: Water in the aboleth's lair magically becomes a conduit for the creature's rage. The aboleth can target any number of creatures it can see in such water within 90 feet of it. A target must succeed on a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or take 7 (2d6) psychic damage. The aboleth can't use this lair action again until it has used a different one.

aboleth.regional_effects_intro: The region containing an aboleth's lair is warped by the creature's presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Water Transmutation
regional_effects.desc: Underground surfaces within 1 mile of the aboleth's lair are slimy and wet and are difficult terrain.

regional_effects.name: Psychic Disturbance
regional_effects.desc: Water sources within 1 mile of the lair are supernaturally fouled. Enemies of the aboleth that drink such water vomit it within minutes.

regional_effects.name: Psychic Projection
regional_effects.desc: As an action, the aboleth can create an illusory image of itself within 1 mile of the lair. The copy can appear at any location the aboleth has seen before or in any location a creature charmed by the aboleth can currently see. Once created, the image persists for as long as the aboleth maintains concentration, as if concentrating on a spell. Although the image is intangible, it looks, sounds, and can move like the aboleth. The aboleth can sense, speak, and use telepathy from the image's position as if present at that position. If the image takes any damage, it disappears.

aboleth.regional_effects_closing: If the aboleth dies, the first two effects fade over the course of 3d10 days.

black-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Murky Pools
lair_actions.desc: Pools of water that the dragon can see within 120 feet of it surge outward in a grasping tide. Any creature on the ground within 20 feet of such a pool must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 20 feet into the water and knocked prone.

lair_actions.name: Swarm of Insects
lair_actions.desc: A cloud of swarming insects fills a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point the dragon chooses within 120 feet of it. The cloud spreads around corners and remains until the dragon dismisses it as an action, uses this lair action again, or dies. The cloud is lightly obscured. Any creature in the cloud when it appears must make on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that ends its turn in the cloud takes 10 (3d6) piercing damage.

lair_actions.name: Magical Darkness
lair_actions.desc: Magical darkness spreads from a point the dragon chooses within 60 feet of it, filling a 15-foot-radius sphere until the dragon dismisses it as an action, uses this lair action again, or dies. The darkness spreads around corners. A creature with darkvision can't see through this darkness, and nonmagical light can't illuminate it. If any of the effect's area overlaps with an area of light created by a spell of 2nd level or lower, the spell that created the light is dispelled.

black-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary black dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Foul Water
regional_effects.desc: Water sources within 1 mile of the lair are supernaturally fouled. Enemies of the dragon that drink such water regurgitate it within minutes.

regional_effects.name: Obscuring Fog
regional_effects.desc: Fog lightly obscures the land within 6 miles of the lair.

regional_effects.name: Reptilian Spies
regional_effects.desc: Reptiles within 6 miles of the lair serve as combative combatants for the dragon. The dragon is aware of these creatures as if it could telepathically communicate with them.

black-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, the fog fades within 2d10 days. The other effects fade over the course of 1d10 days.

blue-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Ceiling Collapse
lair_actions.desc: Part of the ceiling collapses above one creature that the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The creature must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 10 (3d6) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone and buried. The buried target is restrained and unable to breathe or stand up. A creature can take an action to make a DC 10 Strength check, ending the buried state on a success.

lair_actions.name: Sand Cloud
lair_actions.desc: A cloud of sand swirls about in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The cloud spreads around corners. Each creature in the cloud must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be blinded for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

lair_actions.name: Lightning Arc
lair_actions.desc: Lightning arcs, forming a 5-foot-wide line between two of the lair's solid surfaces that the dragon can see. They must be within 120 feet of the dragon and 120 feet of each other. Each creature in that line must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 10 (3d6) lightning damage.

blue-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary blue dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Thunderstorms
regional_effects.desc: Thunderstorms rage within 6 miles of the lair.

regional_effects.name: Dust Devils
regional_effects.desc: Dust devils scour the land within 6 miles of the lair. A dust devil has the statistics of an air elemental, but it can't fly, has a speed of 50 feet, and has an Intelligence and Charisma of 1 (-5).

regional_effects.name: Hidden Sinkholes
regional_effects.desc: Hidden sinkholes form in and around the dragon's lair. A sinkhole can be detected from a safe distance with a successful DC 20 Wisdom (Perception) check. Otherwise, the first creature to step on the thin covering over the sinkhole must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or fall 1d6 x 10 feet into the sinkhole.

blue-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, the thunderstorms abate within 1d10 days, but the other effects persist.

green-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Grasping Roots
lair_actions.desc: Grasping roots and vines erupt in a 20-foot radius centered on a point on the ground that the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. That area becomes difficult terrain, and each creature there must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be restrained by the roots and vines. A creature can be freed if it or another creature takes an action to make a DC 15 Strength check and succeeds. The roots and vines wilt away when the dragon uses this lair action again or when the dragon dies.

lair_actions.name: Thorny Wall
lair_actions.desc: A wall of tangled brush bristling with thorns springs into existence on a solid surface within 120 feet of the dragon. The wall is up to 60 feet long, 10 feet high, and 5 feet thick, and it blocks line of sight. When the wall appears, each creature in its area must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. A creature that fails the save takes 18 (4d8) piercing damage and is pushed 5 feet out of the wall's space, appearing on whichever side of the wall it wants. A creature can move through the wall, albeit slowly and painfully. For every 1 foot a creature travels through the wall, it must spend 4 feet of movement. Furthermore, a creature in the wall's space must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw once each round it's in contact with the wall, taking 18 (4d8) piercing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Each 10-foot section of wall has AC 5, 15 hit points, vulnerability to fire damage, resistance to bludgeoning and piercing damage, and immunity to psychic damage. The wall sinks back into the ground when the dragon uses this lair action again or when the dragon dies.

lair_actions.name: Charming Mist
lair_actions.desc: Magical fog rolls in around one creature the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The creature must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by the dragon until initiative count 20 on the next round.

green-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary green dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Deceptive Mist
regional_effects.desc: Thickets form labyrinthine passages within 1 mile of the dragon's lair. The thickets act as 10-foot-high, 10-foot-thick walls that block line of sight. Creatures can move through the thickets, with every 1 foot of movement through the thickets costing 4 feet of movement. A creature in the thickets must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw once each round it's in contact with the thickets or take 3 (1d6) piercing damage from thorns.

regional_effects.name: Beast Spies
regional_effects.desc: Within 1 mile of its lair, the dragon leaves no physical evidence of its passage unless it wishes to. Tracking it there is impossible except by magical means. In addition, it ignores movement restrictions caused by undergrowth.

regional_effects.name: Luring Paths
regional_effects.desc: Rodents and birds within 1 mile of the dragon's lair serve as the dragon's eyes and ears. Deer and other large game are strangely absent, hinting at the predator's bytes.

green-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, the thickets remain but become ordinary plants. Other effects fade over 1d10 days.

red-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Magma Eruption
lair_actions.desc: Magma erupts from a point on the ground the dragon can see within 120 feet of it, creating a 20-foot-high, 5-foot-radius geyser. Each creature in the geyser's area must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 21 (6d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

lair_actions.name: Tremor
lair_actions.desc: A tremor shakes the lair in a 60-foot radius around the dragon. Each creature other than the dragon on the ground in that area must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone.

lair_actions.name: Volcanic Gas
lair_actions.desc: Volcanic gases form a cloud in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is lightly obscured. It lasts until initiative count 20 on the next round. Each creature that starts its turn in the cloud must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of its turn. While poisoned in this way, a creature is incapacitated.

red-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary red dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Minor Earthquakes
regional_effects.desc: Small earthquakes are common within 6 miles of the dragon's lair.

regional_effects.name: Sulfurous Fumes
regional_effects.desc: Water sources within 1 mile of the lair are supernaturally warm and tainted by sulfur.

regional_effects.name: Rocky Fissures
regional_effects.desc: Rocky fissures within 1 mile of the dragon's lair form portals to the Elemental Plane of Fire, allowing creatures of elemental fire into the world to dwell nearby.

red-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, these effects fade over the course of 1d10 days.

white-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Freezing Fog
lair_actions.desc: Freezing fog fills a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The fog spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. Each creature in the fog when it appears must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature that ends its turn in the fog takes 10 (3d6) cold damage. A wind of at least 20 miles per hour disperses the fog. The fog otherwise lasts until the dragon uses this lair action again or until the dragon dies.

lair_actions.name: Ice Shards
lair_actions.desc: Jagged ice shards fall from the ceiling, striking up to three creatures underneath that the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The dragon makes one ranged attack roll (+7 to hit) against each target. On a hit, the target takes 10 (3d6) piercing damage.

lair_actions.name: Icy Ground
lair_actions.desc: The dragon creates an opaque wall of ice on a solid surface it can see within 120 feet of it. The wall can be up to 30 feet long, 30 feet high, and 1 foot thick. When the wall appears, each creature within its area is pushed 5 feet out of the wall's space; appearing on whichever side of the wall it wants. Each 10-foot section of the wall has AC 5, 30 hit points, vulnerability to fire damage, and immunity to acid, cold, necrotic, poison, and psychic damage. The wall disappears when the dragon uses this lair action again or when the dragon dies.

white-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary white dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Cold Fog
regional_effects.desc: Chilly fog lightly obscures the land within 6 miles of the dragon's lair.

regional_effects.name: Freezing Precipitation
regional_effects.desc: Freezing precipitation falls within 6 miles of the dragon's lair, sometimes forming blizzard conditions when the dragon is at rest.

regional_effects.name: Ice Walls
regional_effects.desc: Icy walls block off areas in the dragon's lair. Each wall is 6 inches thick, and a 10-foot section has AC 5, 15 hit points, vulnerability to fire damage, and immunity to acid, cold, necrotic, poison, and psychic damage.

white-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, the fog and precipitation fade within 1 day. The ice walls melt over the course of 1d10 days.

brass-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Sand Geyser
lair_actions.desc: A strong wind blows around the dragon. Each creature within 60 feet of the dragon must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away from the dragon and knocked prone. Gases and vapors are dispersed by the wind, and unprotected flames are extinguished. Protected flames, such as lanterns, have a 50 percent chance of being extinguished.

lair_actions.name: Sinkhole
lair_actions.desc: A cloud of sand swirls about in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. The cloud spreads around corners. Each creature in the cloud must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be blinded for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

brass-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary brass dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Dust Devils
regional_effects.desc: Dust devils scour the land within 6 miles of the lair. A dust devil has the statistics of an air elemental, but it can't fly, has a speed of 50 feet, and has an Intelligence and Charisma of 1 (-5).

regional_effects.name: Food Spoilage
regional_effects.desc: Food and water within 1 mile of the dragon's lair magically spoil within 1 hour of being prepared unless the dragon or its allies wish otherwise.

regional_effects.name: Mirages
regional_effects.desc: Images of Large or larger creatures haunt the desert sands within 6 miles of the dragon's lair. These illusions move and appear real, although they can do no harm. A creature that examines an image from a distance can tell it's an illusion with a successful DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) check. Any physical interaction with an image reveals it to be an illusion, because objects pass through it.

brass-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, the dust devils disappear immediately, and the illusions fade over 1d10 days. Other effects fade over 1d4 months.

bronze-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects:

lair_actions.name: Fog Cloud
lair_actions.desc: The dragon creates fog as though it had cast the fog cloud spell. The fog lasts until initiative count 20 on the next round.

lair_actions.name: Thunderclap
lair_actions.desc: A thunderclap originates at a point the dragon can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature within a 20-foot radius centered on that point must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or take 5 (1d10) thunder damage and be deafened until the end of its next turn.

bronze-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary bronze dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic.

regional_effects.name: Dense Fog
regional_effects.desc: Once per day, the dragon can alter the weather in a 6-mile radius centered on its lair. The dragon doesn't need to be outdoors; otherwise the effect is identical to the control weather spell.

regional_effects.name: Rough Seas
regional_effects.desc: Underwater plants within 6 miles of the dragon's lair take on dazzlingly brilliant hues.

regional_effects.name: Watery Shapes
regional_effects.desc: Within its lair, the dragon can set illusory sounds, such as soft music and strange echoes, so that they can be heard in various parts of the lair.

bronze-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, changed weather reverts to normal, as described in the spell, and the other effects fade in 1d10 days.

copper-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Mud Spike
lair_actions.desc: The dragon chooses a point on the ground that it can see within 120 feet of it. Stone spikes erupt from the ground in a 20-foot radius centered on that point. The effect is otherwise identical to the spike growth spell and lasts until the dragon uses this lair action again or until the dragon dies.

lair_actions.name: Stone Wall Shift
lair_actions.desc: The dragon chooses a 10-foot-square area on the ground that it can see within 120 feet of it. The ground in that area turns into 3-foot-deep mud. Each creature on the ground in that area when the mud appears must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or sink into the mud and become restrained. A creature can take an action to attempt a DC 15 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach from the mud on a success. The mud dries over the course of 10 minutes, after which a creature restrained by the mud is no longer restrained.

copper-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary copper dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Dead Ends
regional_effects.desc: Magic carvings of the dragon's smiling face can be googled the walls at dead ends and intersections, flickering to life with a warm glow, allowing the dragon to observe interlopers.

regional_effects.name: Hallucinations
regional_effects.desc: Small earthquakes are common within 6 miles of the dragon's lair.

regional_effects.name: Tricky Walls
regional_effects.desc: Whenever a creature with an Intelligence of 3 or higher comes within 30 feet of a water source within 1 mile of the dragon's lair, the dragon becomes aware of the creature's presence and location.

copper-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, the magic carvings fade over the course of 1d10 days.

gold-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Foretelling
lair_actions.desc: The dragon glimpses the future, so it has advantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws until initiative count 20 on the next round.

lair_actions.name: Banishment
lair_actions.desc: One creature the dragon can see within 120 feet of it must succeed on a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or be banished to a dream plane, a different plane of existence the dragon has imagined into being. To escape, the creature must use its action to make a DC 15 Charisma check. If the creature succeeds, it escapes the dream plane. Otherwise, the effect ends on initiative count 20 on the next round. When the effect ends, the creature reappears in the space it left or in the nearest unoccupied space if that one is occupied.

gold-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary gold dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Gentle Warmth
regional_effects.desc: Whenever a creature that can understand a language sleeps or enters a state of trance or reverie within 6 miles of the dragon's lair, the dragon can establish telepathic contact with that creature and converse with it in its dreams. The creature remembers its conversation with the dragon upon waking.

regional_effects.name: Gem Deposits
regional_effects.desc: Banks of beautiful mist pervade the lair within 6 miles of it. The mist doesn't obscure anything. It assumes haunting forms when evil creatures are within 1 mile of the dragon's lair, warning good creatures of the evil's presence.

regional_effects.name: Blessed Water
regional_effects.desc: Gems and pearls within 1 mile of the dragon's lair sparkle and gleam, shedding dim light in a 5-foot radius and a faint, otherworldly music drifts through the air.

gold-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, these effects end immediately.

silver-dragon.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the dragon takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the dragon can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Freezing Fog
lair_actions.desc: The dragon creates fog as if it had cast the fog cloud spell. The fog lasts until initiative count 20 on the next round.

lair_actions.name: Icy Wall
lair_actions.desc: The dragon creates an opaque wall of ice on a solid surface it can see within 120 feet of it. The wall can be up to 30 feet long, 30 feet high, and 1 foot thick. When the wall appears, each creature within its area is pushed 5 feet out of the wall's space, appearing on whichever side of the wall it wants. Each 10-foot section of the wall has AC 5, 30 hit points, vulnerability to fire damage, and immunity to acid, cold, necrotic, poison, and psychic damage. The wall disappears when the dragon uses this lair action again or when the dragon dies.

silver-dragon.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a legendary silver dragon's lair is warped by the dragon's magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Chilling Fog
regional_effects.desc: The land within 6 miles of the lair experiences harsh weather: blinding blizzards, bitter cold snaps, or sparkling snowfalls.

regional_effects.name: Magic Wards
regional_effects.desc: Within its lair, the dragon can set illusory sounds, such as soft music and strange echoes, so that they can be heard in various parts of the lair.

regional_effects.name: Enchanted Snow
regional_effects.desc: Within 1 mile of the lair, winter chill is oddly comfortable rather than numbing, and the snow is pristine and beautiful.

silver-dragon.regional_effects_closing: If the dragon dies, changed weather reverts to normal over the course of 1d10 days, and the other effects fade over 1d10 days.

kraken.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the kraken takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the kraken can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Strong Current
lair_actions.desc: A strong current moves through the kraken's lair. Each creature within 60 feet of the kraken must succeed on a DC 23 Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 60 feet away from the kraken. On a success, the creature is pushed 10 feet away from the kraken.

lair_actions.name: Lightning Storm
lair_actions.desc: Creatures in the water within 60 feet of the kraken have vulnerability to lightning damage until initiative count 20 on the next round.

lair_actions.name: Water Arm
lair_actions.desc: The water in the kraken's lair becomes electrically charged. All creatures within 120 feet of the kraken must succeed on a DC 23 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

kraken.regional_effects_intro: The region containing a kraken's lair is warped by the creature's blasphemous presence, creating the following magical effects:

regional_effects.name: Weather Control
regional_effects.desc: The kraken can alter the weather at will in a 6-mile radius centered on its lair. The effect is otherwise identical to the control weather spell.

regional_effects.name: Aquatic Creatures
regional_effects.desc: Water elementals coalesce within 6 miles of the lair. These elementals can't leave the water and have Intelligence and Charisma scores of 1 (-5).

regional_effects.name: Underwater Visibility
regional_effects.desc: The kraken can sense the thoughts of any creature that is in water within 6 miles of the kraken's lair. It can also affect such creatures with its Telepathy trait.

kraken.regional_effects_closing: When the kraken dies, all these regional effects fade immediately.

lich.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the lich can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the lich can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Death Emanation
lair_actions.desc: The lich rolls a d8 and regains a spell slot of that level or lower. If it has no spent spell slots of that level or lower, nothing happens.

lair_actions.name: Spirit Drain
lair_actions.desc: The lich targets one creature it can see within 30 feet of it. A crackling cord of negative energy tethers the lich to the target. Whenever the lich takes damage, the target must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the lich takes half the damage (rounded down), and the target takes the remaining damage. This tether lasts until initiative count 20 on the next round or until the lich or the target is no longer in the lich's lair.

lair_actions.name: Undead Summon
lair_actions.desc: The lich calls forth the spirits of creatures that died in its lair. These apparitions materialize and attack one creature that the lich can see within 60 feet of it. The target must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw, taking 52 (15d6) necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a success. The apparitions then disappear.

lich.regional_effects_intro: A lich's lair might have any or all of the following magical effects in place:

regional_effects.name: Undead Corruption
regional_effects.desc: Creatures within 1 mile of the lich's lair that die become undead skeletons or zombies (the lich's choice) under the lich's control after 1d10 days.

regional_effects.name: Intelligence Network
regional_effects.desc: The lich can take actions to project its awareness to a 5-mile radius around its lair. The lich can't be surprised while it has this awareness.

regional_effects.name: Planar Shift
regional_effects.desc: If a creature friendly to the lich is reduced to 0 hit points within the lair, that creature automatically stabilizes and gains 1 hit point in 1d4 hours.

lich.regional_effects_closing: If the lich is destroyed but its phylactery remains, these effects persist until the phylactery is also destroyed.

unicorn.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the unicorn can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the unicorn can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Shimmer of Protection
lair_actions.desc: The unicorn causes shimmering lights to appear in a 30-foot radius centered on a point it can see within 120 feet of it. Each creature of the unicorn's choice in that area gains 10 temporary hit points.

lair_actions.name: Healing Spring
lair_actions.desc: The unicorn targets one creature it can see within 60 feet of it. A shimmering spring of healing energy wells up beneath the target, restoring 10 (3d6) hit points to the target and ending one disease or one of the following conditions: blinded, deafened, paralyzed, or poisoned.

lair_actions.name: Calm Emotions
lair_actions.desc: The unicorn casts calm emotions with a DC 15 saving throw. The spell doesn't require concentration when cast this way.

unicorn.regional_effects_intro: A unicorn's presence sanctifies its forest home. The following magical effects are linked to the unicorn's lair:

regional_effects.name: Memory Loss
regional_effects.desc: Open flames of a nonmagical nature are extinguished within the unicorn's domain. Torches and campfires refuse to burn, but closed lanterns are unaffected.

regional_effects.name: No Tracking
regional_effects.desc: Creatures native to the unicorn's domain have an easier time hiding; they have advantage on all Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide.

regional_effects.name: Endless Sustenance
regional_effects.desc: When a good-aligned creature casts a spell or uses a magical effect that causes another good-aligned creature to regain hit points, the target regains the maximum number of hit points possible for the spell or effect.

unicorn.regional_effects_closing: If the unicorn dies, these effects fade over the course of 1d10 days.

beholder.lair_actions_intro: When fighting inside its lair, a beholder can invoke the ambient magic to take lair actions. On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the beholder can take one lair action to cause one of the following effects. The beholder can't repeat an effect until they have all been used, and it can't use the same effect two rounds in a row.

lair_actions.name: Slimy Ground
lair_actions.desc: A 50-foot-square area of ground within 120 feet of the beholder becomes slimy; that area is difficult terrain until initiative count 20 on the next round.

lair_actions.name: Grasping Appendages
lair_actions.desc: Walls within 120 feet of the beholder sprout grasping appendages until initiative count 20 on the round after next. Each creature of the beholder's choice that starts its turn within 10 feet of such a wall must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or be grappled. Escaping requires a successful DC 15 Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check.

lair_actions.name: Wandering Eye
lair_actions.desc: An eye opens on a solid surface within 60 feet of the beholder. One random eye ray of the beholder shoots from that eye at a target of the beholder's choice that it can see. The eye then closes and disappears.

beholder.regional_effects_intro: A region containing a beholder's lair is warped by the creature's unnatural presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Paranoia
regional_effects.desc: Creatures within 1 mile of the beholder's lair sometimes feel as if they're being watched when they aren't.

regional_effects.name: Reality Warps
regional_effects.desc: When the beholder sleeps, minor warps in reality occur within 1 mile of its lair and then vanish 24 hours later. Marks on cave walls might change subtly, an eerie trinket might appear where none existed before, harmless slime might coat a statue, and so on. These effects apply only to natural surfaces and to nonmagical objects that aren't on anyone's person.

beholder.regional_effects_closing: If the beholder dies, these effects fade over the course of 1d10 days.

mummy-lord.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the mummy lord takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects; the mummy lord can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

lair_actions.name: Undead Awareness
lair_actions.desc: Each undead creature in the lair can pinpoint the location of each living creature within 120 feet of it until initiative count 20 on the next round.

lair_actions.name: Turn Resistance
lair_actions.desc: Each undead in the lair has advantage on saving throws against effects that turn undead until initiative count 20 on the next round.

lair_actions.name: Spell Disruption
lair_actions.desc: Until initiative count 20 on the next round, any non-undead creature that tries to cast a spell of 4th level or lower in the mummy lord's lair is wracked with pain. The creature can choose another action, but if it tries to cast the spell, it must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 1d6 necrotic damage per level of the spell, and the spell has no effect and is wasted.

mummy-lord.regional_effects_intro: A mummy lord's temple or tomb is warped in any of the following ways by the creature's dark presence:

regional_effects.name: Food Decay
regional_effects.desc: Food instantly molders and water instantly evaporates when brought into the lair. Other nonmagical drinks are spoiled — wine turning to vinegar, for instance.

regional_effects.name: Divination Interference
regional_effects.desc: Divination spells cast within the lair by creatures other than the mummy lord have a 25 percent chance to provide misleading results, as determined by the DM. If a divination spell already has a chance to fail or become unreliable when cast multiple times, that chance increases by 25 percent.

regional_effects.name: Treasure Curse
regional_effects.desc: A creature that takes treasure from the lair is cursed until the treasure is returned. The cursed target has disadvantage on all saving throws. The curse lasts until removed by a remove curse spell or other magic.

mummy-lord.regional_effects_closing: If the mummy lord is destroyed, these regional effects end immediately.

sphinx.lair_actions_intro: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the sphinx can take a lair action to cause one of the following magical effects; the sphinx can't use an effect again until it finishes a short or long rest:

lair_actions.name: Time Reroll
lair_actions.desc: The flow of time is altered such that every creature in the lair must reroll initiative. The sphinx can choose not to reroll.

lair_actions.name: Age Shift
lair_actions.desc: The effects of time are altered such that every creature in the lair must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become 1d20 years older or younger (the sphinx's choice), but never any younger than 1 year old. A greater restoration spell can restore a creature's age to normal.

lair_actions.name: Time Warp
lair_actions.desc: The flow of time within the lair is altered such that everything within moves up to 10 years forward or backward (sphinx's choice). Only the sphinx is immediately aware of the time change. A wish spell can return the caster and up to seven other creatures designated by the caster to their normal time.

lair_actions.name: Plane Shift
lair_actions.desc: The sphinx shifts itself and up to seven other creatures it can see within its lair to another plane of existence. Once outside its lair, the sphinx can't use lair actions, but it can return to its lair as a bonus action on its turn, taking up to seven creatures with it.

vampire.regional_effects_intro: The region surrounding a vampire's lair is warped by the creature's unnatural presence, creating any of the following effects:

regional_effects.name: Vermin Surge
regional_effects.desc: There's a noticeable increase in the populations of bats, rats, and wolves in the region.

regional_effects.name: Withered Plants
regional_effects.desc: Plants within 500 feet of the lair wither, and their stems and branches become twisted and thorny.

regional_effects.name: Living Shadows
regional_effects.desc: Shadows cast within 500 feet of the lair seem abnormally gaunt and sometimes move as though alive.

regional_effects.name: Creeping Fog
regional_effects.desc: A creeping fog clings to the ground within 500 feet of the vampire's lair. The fog occasionally takes eerie forms, such as grasping claws and writhing serpents.

vampire.regional_effects_closing: If the vampire is destroyed, these effects end after 2d6 days.
